description: This stacked bar chart presents the number percentage distribution of inclusions grouped by four size categories (<2 μm, 2-4 μm, 4-6 μm, >6 μm) in two Fe–28Mn–10Al–0.8C low-density steel samples: low-nitrogen (LN) steel and high-nitrogen (HN) steel. For LN steel, inclusions are distributed as 39% <2 μm, 39% 2-4 μm, 12% 4-6 μm, and 10% >6 μm, with 78% of inclusions smaller than 4 μm. For HN steel, 84% of inclusions (dominantly AlN) are <2 μm, 11% 2-4 μm, 4% 4-6 μm, with no inclusions larger than 6 μm, so 95% of inclusions are smaller than 4 μm. The comparison demonstrates that nitrogen addition drastically increases the proportion of fine inclusions under 2 μm and reduces average inclusion size. This evidence supports that fine dispersed AlN inclusions act as heterogeneous nucleation sites to refine austenite grains, contributing to enhanced mechanical properties of HN steel.
